top_signals.title: Brent Oil Remains On Track for 8% Weekly Decline as Israel, Hezbollah Agree to Ceasefire
top_signals.summary: Brent crude ticked slightly higher on Friday but was still on track for a more than 8% weekly decline after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire and tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased.Brent crude futures were up 20 cents, or 0.25%, at $80.05 a barrel by 10:55 a.m.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: How China’s economic comeback hit a wall
top_signals.summary: Activity is sputtering midway through 2026, undercutting efforts to galvanise the economy
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
top_signals.summary: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ‘Most difficult day in the history of digital credit’: Strive CEO says leverage liquidation drove STRC and SATA selloff
top_signals.summary: STRC and SATA, designed to trade near a $100 par value, fell significantly on Thursday before partially recovering.
